children.heading: Goals
children.label: 1 Goals
children.text: There is established, as part of the Corps of Engineers water resources development program, an interim goal of no overall net loss of the Nation’s remaining wetlands base, as defined by acreage and function, and a long-term goal to increase the quality and quantity of the Nation’s wetlands, as defined by acreage and function.

children.heading: Use of authorities
children.label: 2 Use of authorities
children.text: The Secretary shall utilize all appropriate authorities, including those to restore and create wetlands, in meeting the interim and long-term goals.

children.heading: Development
children.label: A Development
children.text: The Secretary shall develop, in consultation with the Environmental Protection Agency, the Fish and Wildlife Service, and other appropriate Federal agencies, a wetlands action plan to achieve the goals established by this subsection as soon as possible.

children.heading: Contents
children.label: B Contents
children.text: The plan shall include and identify actions to be taken by the Secretary in achieving the goals and any new authorities which may be necessary to accelerate attainment of the goals.

children.heading: Completion deadline
children.label: C Completion deadline
children.text: The Secretary shall complete the plan not later than 1 year after .November 28, 1990

children.heading: Constructed wetlands for Mud Creek, Arkansas
children.label: b Constructed wetlands for Mud Creek, Arkansas

children.text: the use of constructed wetlands for wastewater treatment, and

children.label: A
children.text: to meeting the objective of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act [ et seq.] to restore and maintain the physical, chemical, and biological integrity of the Nation’s waters, and33 U.S.C. 1251

children.label: B
children.text: to attaining the goals established by subsection (a).

to provide, without cost to the United States, all lands, easements, rights-of-way, relocations, and dredged material disposal areas necessary for construction and subsequent research and demonstration work;

to hold and save the United States free from damages due to construction, operation, and maintenance of the project, except damages due to the fault or negligence of the United States or its contractors; and

to operate and maintain the restored or constructed wetlands in accordance with good management practices; except that nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as precluding a Federal agency from agreeing to operate and maintain the restored or reconstructed wetlands.

children.heading: Wetlands restoration and enhancement demonstration program
children.label: d Wetlands restoration and enhancement demonstration program

children.heading: Establishment and implementation
children.label: 1 Establishment and implementation
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Administrator, is authorized to establish and implement a demonstration program for the purpose of determining the feasibility of wetlands restoration, enhancement, and creation as a means of contributing to the goals established by subsection (a).

children.heading: Goal
children.label: 2 Goal
children.text: The goal of the program under this subsection shall be to establish a limited number of demonstration wetlands restoration, enhancement, and creation areas in districts of the Corps of Engineers for the purpose of evaluating the technical and scientific long-term feasibility of such areas as a means of contributing to the attainment of the goals established by subsection (a). Federal and State land-owning agencies and private parties may contribute to such areas.

children.label: A
children.text: past experience with wetlands restoration, enhancement, and creation;

children.label: B
children.text: the appropriate means of measuring benefits of compensatory mitigation activities, including enhancement or restoration of existing wetlands or creation of wetlands;

children.label: C
children.text: the appropriate geographic scope for which wetlands loss may be offset by restoration, enhancement, and creation efforts;

children.label: D
children.text: the technical feasibility and scientific likelihood that wetlands can be successfully restored, enhanced, and created;

children.label: E
children.text: means of establishing liability for, and long-term ownership of, wetlands restoration, enhancement, and creation areas; and

children.label: F
children.text: responsibilities for short- and long-term project monitoring.

children.heading: To the Chief of Engineers
children.label: A To the Chief of Engineers
children.text: The district engineer for each district of the Corps of Engineers in which a wetlands restoration, enhancement, and creation area is established under this subsection shall transmit annual reports to the Chief of Engineers describing the amount and value of wetlands restored, enhanced, and created for the area and a summary of whether the area is contributing to the goal established in paragraph (2).

children.heading: To Congress
children.label: B To Congress
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary shall transmit to Congress a report evaluating the use of wetlands restoration, enhancement, and creation areas in fulfilling the goal established by paragraph (2), together with recommendations on whether or not to continue use of such areas as a means of meeting the goals established by subsection (a).November 28, 1990

children.heading: Effect on other laws
children.label: 5 Effect on other laws
children.text: Nothing in this subsection affects any requirements under section 404 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act () or .33 U.S.C. 1344section 403 of this title

children.heading: Training and certification of delineators
children.label: e Training and certification of delineators

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to establish a program for the training and certification of individuals as wetlands delineators. As part of such program, the Secretary shall carry out demonstration projects in districts of the Corps of Engineers. The program shall include training and certification of delineators and procedures for expediting consideration and acceptance of delineations performed by certified delineators.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 2 Reports
children.text: The Secretary shall transmit to Congress periodic reports concerning the status of the program and any recommendations on improving the content and implementation of the Federal Manual for Identifying and Delineating Jurisdictional Wetlands.
